Blockchain technology getting Stanford treatment.
press release distributed Stanford Engineering June 20th revealed that Stanford computer scientists have founded Center Blockchain Research Stanford University.
initiative professors computer science, Boneh David Mazières, will dedicated researching understanding technology that promises fundamentally change people companies make deals complete financial transactions over internet," release states.
release also revealed that center will working with industry leaders Ethereum Foundation, Protocol Labs OmiseGO, among others addition university scientists, with goal creating "best practices" blockchain industry.
center will conduct research aimed answering many blockchain's most pressing questions, including "how scale technology billions users; ensure that data trustworthy accurate while still keeping certain details private; maintain high levels security without using much computing resources that cost electricity becomes limiting factor applications," release states.

Years since first course, going multiple follow intensive courses, attending every single monthly refresher day, joining toastmasters, attending dozens interviews (not just job, managed great ones, because actually having doing it), running businesses, being McGP Coach, Course Instructor Regional Director, truly went beyond stuttering, always find ways push comfort zones whenever can.

Today OpenEBS announced that Evan Powell, co-founder companies including Nexenta Systems, leader open storage, StackStorm, leader DevOps automation, joined CEO.
addition, OpenEBS achieved major milestone with release available trial hyper-converged solution containerized environments.
OpenEBS enables containers mission critical workloads delivering storage functionality needed these applications, such ensuring they receive performance, data protection, storage capacity required.
OpenEBS unique amongst storage systems being built with same building blocks that comprise today's highly agile DevOps environments, including microservices, containers, leading open source cloud orchestrators; this approach allows OpenEBS containers running mission critical workloads which supports managed just like other container, saving time resources reducing risk while unlocking agility DevOps environments.
OpenEBS (@openebs) addresses challenge large scale storage management faced many container users they move mission critical, stateful workloads onto containers running multicloud hybrid environment.
OpenEBS works seamlessly with Kubernetes, Docker other orchestration projects itself built cloud native way.
OpenEBS available under Apache license.
CloudByte provider Software defined storage software appliances.
CloudByte's patented technology been helping large storage deployments realize significant cost savings well controls simplicity management.
Established 2011, CloudByte development offices India, Europe, customers world-wide.
